title: T3.1: Parquet result schema (replace per-iter JSON outputs)
description: Current composition_records.json + workflow_records.json + identification_records.json per iter = ~50 MB JSON × 48 iters = 2.4 GB and hard to query. Write everything to a single Parquet file per experiment (or per cell) with a schema that includes run metadata (cell, identifier, platform, seed, ...). Acceptance: aggregated parquet is <100 MB total, supports DuckDB / pandas queries that group by cell × identifier × seed in milliseconds.
close_reason:
Completed in PR #126 (merged ba1f043 to dev).

Replaced per-iter JSON record dumps with single results.parquet per
experiment. Key deliverables:

* cflibs/benchmark/results.py — new write_parquet / read_parquet
  module with unified row schema, run-metadata columns, append +
  optional hive partitioning, posterior diagnostics extraction.
* UnifiedBenchmarkRunner.write_outputs now takes output_format=
  ('parquet'|'json'|'both', default 'parquet') and run_metadata kwargs.
  Env override CFLIBS_OUTPUT_FORMAT. Falls back to JSON if pyarrow
  unavailable.
* scripts/run_unified_benchmark.py: --output-format,
  --experiment-label, --cell, --seed, --iter-index, --platform.
* docs/results-parquet-schema.md (full schema reference, query
  examples). docs/parquet-results-consultation.md (design rationale).
* tests/benchmark/test_results_parquet.py — 13 new tests, all 193
  benchmark tests still pass.

Acceptance verified:
  - 2,880-row synthetic 48-iter experiment = 34 KB (budget <100 MB).
  - DuckDB GROUP BY identifier = 2.6 ms (budget <100 ms).
  - 100% of IDEvaluationRecord + CompositionEvaluationRecord fields
    round-trip.
  - Schema documented in docs/results-parquet-schema.md.

title: T2.4: Pre-built basis libraries shared via NFS
description: Basis-driven identifiers (spectral_nnls, nnls_concentration_threshold) need output/basis_libraries/ which currently doesn't exist on vasp-01/02. Build the basis libraries once via scripts/build_basis_library.py (or equivalent), commit to NFS at /cluster/shared/cf-libs-bench/basis_libraries, default --basis-dir to point there. Acceptance: spectral_nnls identifier successfully runs on all 3 nodes without per-node basis rebuild.
notes: First build crashed at 04:20 on first .h5 write with PermissionError — slurm-ctl had drwxr-sr-x root:hpc and NFS root_squash maps vasp-02 root to nobody. Fixed with chmod 1777 /cluster/shared/cf-libs-bench/basis_libraries on slurm-ctl (sticky+world-writable). Write test confirmed. Restarted at 10:42Z (PID 158661 on vasp-02), grinding from FWHM 0.05 element 1/83. Will close once 8 .h5 files + manifest.json land on NFS.
close_reason: Code (PR #127, squash 3063a0c) + artifact both live. /cluster/shared/cf-libs-bench/basis_libraries/ now contains 8 FWHM .h5 files (51-204 MB, 995 MB total) + manifest.json. md5sum ed9e3a9a42296bacf8852475d5744b89 verified identical across vasp-01/02/03. Build wall: ~80 min on vasp-02 CPU. First attempt crashed on NFS write permission (root_squash + drwxr-sr-x); fixed with chmod 1777 on slurm-ctl, restarted, completed successfully. Acceptance met: spectral_nnls / hybrid_intersect / hybrid_union / nnls_concentration_threshold identifiers now have basis files on all 3 vasp nodes without per-node rebuild.

title: T2.3: Multi-armed bandit iteration allocator (Thompson sampling)
description: After a warmup phase (N=2 iters per cell), use Thompson sampling or UCB to re-allocate remaining iterations to the cells with the most promising d_A trajectories. Same total compute, far more bootstrap precision on top performers, fewer wasted iters on clearly-worse configs. Acceptance: in a fixed compute budget, bandit-allocated runs produce tighter CI on the best cell than equal-allocation does.
close_reason: PR #131 merged-pending: cflibs.bandit.ThompsonAllocator + parameter_sweep --bandit/--cells. 94.4% to good arms / 41.6% CI tighter on synthetic 6-arm x 48-iter x 20-seed. 22 tests in tests/bandit/. --bandit 0 byte-identical to T1.1.

title: T2.1: Long-lived cflibs.parameter_sweep_server worker daemon
description: Build a daemon that runs on each node, accepts config requests over a Unix socket / TCP, executes one analysis per request using a shared (preloaded) dataset + JAX JIT cache + atomic DB connection. Iter overhead becomes just analysis time + RPC round-trip. Cold start once per *experiment*, not per *iter*. Acceptance: a sequence of 8 config requests over the socket completes in <1/4 the wall time of 8 separate run_unified_benchmark.py invocations.
close_reason: Daemon shipped via PR #128 (squash 94c9cfb83a). Files: cflibs/parameter_sweep_server/{__init__,protocol,server,client}.py + scripts/start_sweep_server.sh + tests/parameter_sweep_server/{test_protocol,test_e2e}.py + docs/parameter-sweep-server-consultation.md. 16/16 fast tests pass; slow microbench validates the bd <1/4 wall-time gate on real data when ASD_da+data/ present. Live benchmark on bhvo2_usgs: boot 2.6s, REQ1 21s cold-JIT, REQ2/3 ~11s warm-JIT, 1.55x speedup at n=3. Cross-model consult (Codex + Opus 4.7) captured at docs/parameter-sweep-server-consultation.md.

comments.author: Brian Squires
comments.text: PR https://github.com/TheFermiSea/CF-LIBS-improved/pull/128 opened. 16/16 fast tests pass; slow microbench auto-skipped without real data (validated on bhvo2_usgs live: 1.55x at n=3 — boot 2.6s, REQ1 21s cold-JIT, REQ2/3 ~11s warm-JIT). Awaiting squash-merge.

title: T2.2: --dataset-shard arg for split-Vrabel parallel processing
description: Split Vrabel 50k spectra across nodes (e.g., shard 1/3, 2/3, 3/3 = 16.7k each). Each node processes its shard at full sample-richness within the 30-min timeout. Aggregator merges the per-shard results into a unified benchmark report. Acceptance: a 3-node coordinated run produces the same metrics (within bootstrap CI) as a single-node full-Vrabel run.
close_reason: Merged via PR #130 (squash commit 9e6a4358). End-to-end smoke test confirms disjoint cover invariant against real Vrabel HDF5: 3 shards split 200 spectra into [67,67,66], pairwise disjoint, union = full corpus.

comments.author: Brian Squires
comments.text:
Implementation complete; PR open at https://github.com/TheFermiSea/CF-LIBS-improved/pull/130

Acceptance: --dataset-shard N/K flag (1<=N<=K<=16), stride-based partition in cflibs.benchmark.loaders.apply_dataset_shard (applied AFTER per-sample shot cap), scripts/aggregate_shards.py with fail-fast disjoint-cover invariant, Parquet schema v1 + shard_n/shard_k cols, 25/25 new tests pass, end-to-end Vrabel smoke confirms pairwise disjoint shards / union = full corpus / balanced sizes [67,67,66]. Pre-existing test_parameter_sweep failures verified to also fail on dev tip (not a regression).

title: Fix Transition.__init__ missing g_i in tests/inversion/test_comb_fp_reduction.py
description: Pre-existing test failures on dev tip, independently confirmed by 3 separate subagents (PR #119, #120, #122). The Transition constructor signature drifted somewhere (missing or renamed g_i kwarg) and the test was not updated. Reproduces on bare dev with no changes: pytest tests/inversion/test_comb_fp_reduction.py -v shows 2 fails of the form 'Transition.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: g_i'. Investigation needed: which Transition class is used (cflibs.atomic vs cflibs.inversion), what was the recent API change, and whether the test or the Transition is wrong. Not blocking any PR but pollutes regression sweeps.

title: validation-framework: adversarial-twin benchmark run
description:
Per docs/VALIDATION_METRICS.md §6, an adversarial-twin parallel agent
is told the metric AND told to game it. If the twin's gains match a
candidate PR's, the candidate is presumed gamed and rejected.

## Why

The strongest Goodhart hedge: explicitly construct an adversary that
WILL find loopholes. If a candidate's improvement is reproducible by
a metric-aware adversary, it's almost certainly gaming, not science.
Anthropic / OpenAI 2024-2025 reward-hacking literature documents this
as the only effective defense against capable agents.

## Scope (large; deferred)

Requires: a separate orchestrator instance with a "game-the-metric"
profile, prompt-engineering work, and infrastructure for parallel
benchmark dispatch. Scope ~1 week of work.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] An adversarial profile in beefcake-swarm config/profiles.yaml
- [ ] An adversarial benchmark runner that reports per-metric gains
- [ ] PR-gate logic: if adversarial twin's gains overlap candidate's
  by > 50%, block merge
- [ ] Documented edge cases (what if both fail? what if adversarial
  also produces a real improvement?)
notes: swarm opened PR: https://github.com/TheFermiSea/CF-LIBS-improved/pull/88

title: Fix Performance Tests workflow: emit benchmark-results.json
description: .github/workflows/performance.yml run 24967840623 on commit 1a8cb03 fails the upload step with 'Cannot stat benchmark-results.json' — the upstream pytest-benchmark step exits without producing the expected JSON. Investigate the benchmark runner config (pytest --benchmark-json=benchmark-results.json), make sure the output path matches the upload step, or skip the upload step when benchmarks were not collected. Effort: small. No production impact, just removes a recurring red CI badge on main.

title: Fix Documentation workflow first-deploy: bootstrap gh-pages branch
description: .github/workflows/docs.yml fails on push to main because the gh-pages branch does not exist yet (run 24967840626 on commit 1a8cb03 errored 'Remote branch gh-pages not found in upstream origin'). Either (a) bootstrap an empty gh-pages branch, or (b) add force_orphan: true to the peaceiris/actions-gh-pages step so the first deploy creates it. Effort: small (one PR or one git push of an empty orphan branch). Affects no tests, but the failing badge is noise.

title: Auto-skip requires_rust + requires_db markers via conftest hook
description: tests/conftest.py:34 currently auto-skips requires_jax / requires_bayesian / requires_uncertainty when their dependency probe fails (closed CF-LIBS-improved-48c2). Extend the same probe pattern to requires_rust (probe: cflibs.native.cflibs_core or whatever the rust-bridge module is) and requires_db (probe: ASD_da/libs_production.db existing). Currently swarm/CI silently passes by excluding these markers in pytest filters; making the conftest the single source of truth removes the workflow drift. Effort: small. Acceptance: pytest tests/ with no extra flags skips Rust/DB tests cleanly when the deps are missing, runs them when present.

title: test_bayesian::TestTwoZoneMCMCSampler::test_smoke_run fails without NumPyro (missing requires_bayesian marker)
description:
Pre-existing failure on main. When NumPyro is not installed, TwoZoneMCMCSampler.__init__ raises ImportError('NumPyro required. Install with: pip install numpyro'). The test does not guard with a pytest.importorskip or the @requires_bayesian marker that similar Bayesian tests use, so it fails in environments without NumPyro.

Fix options:
1. Add pytestmark = pytest.mark.requires_bayesian at the TestTwoZoneMCMCSampler class level.
2. Guard with pytest.importorskip('numpyro') at the test-function or class level.
3. Make NumPyro a hard dependency of cflibs.inversion.solve.bayesian (not ideal — Bayesian is optional per pyproject.toml).

Reproduction:
  .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_bayesian.py::TestTwoZoneMCMCSampler::test_smoke_run

Error:
  ImportError: NumPyro required. Install with: pip install numpyro

This is not a regression from swarm/3fy3 — verified by running on main.
close_reason: Fixed on swarm/3fy3 @ b274b58. Added pytest_collection_modifyitems hook in tests/conftest.py that turns requires_jax/requires_bayesian/requires_uncertainty markers into actual auto-skip when the optional dep is missing. Test now reports SKIPPED instead of ImportError. Located + edited end-to-end via Serena MCP.

title: Replace magic numbers with named constants in cflibs/core/constants.py
description: ruff PLR2004 flags magic values in physics calculations. Extract commonly used physical constants into named variables in cflibs/core/constants.py. Focus on Boltzmann constant (eV/K), Planck constant (eV*s), and speed of light (nm/s) usages across cflibs/plasma/.

description: ruff D102 reports undocumented public methods in cflibs/radiation/emissivity.py. Add NumPy-style docstrings with Parameters, Returns, and Notes sections. The module handles spectral line emissivity calculation (epsilon = hc/4pi*lambda * A_ki * n_k).
close_reason: Closed

title: Sort imports in cflibs/inversion/boltzmann.py with isort/ruff
description: ruff I001 reports unsorted imports in cflibs/inversion/boltzmann.py. Reorder imports to follow PEP 8 convention: stdlib, third-party, local. Run ruff check --select I cflibs/inversion/boltzmann.py to verify.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add return type annotations to cflibs/plasma/saha_boltzmann.py public functions
description: ruff ANN201 reports missing return type annotations on public functions in cflibs/plasma/saha_boltzmann.py. Add type annotations (-> ReturnType) to all public methods. Use numpy types (npt.NDArray) where appropriate. Run ruff check cflibs/plasma/saha_boltzmann.py to verify.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add docstring to cflibs/radiation/profiles.py voigt_profile function
description: The voigt_profile() function in cflibs/radiation/profiles.py implements Voigt profile convolution but lacks a docstring. Add a NumPy-style docstring with Parameters, Returns, and Notes sections. Include the physics: Voigt = Gaussian (Doppler) * Lorentzian (Stark/natural) convolution.
close_reason: Closed

value: ADR-0001 implementation LANDED ON dev at 64d5e77 (2026-05-13). Final merge from feat/adr-0001-pattern-survey-impl. All six Tier-1 beads closed: 5oar (T1-1 host/kernel infra), 14p6 (T1-3 lax.while_loop), e5o8 (T1-4 LDM broadening), swgm (T1-2 unified forward_model), ke4z (T1-5 chunked scan + checkpoint), 0mor (T1-6 bayesian/ decomposition). §6 gate: 1891 pass / 16 fail / 90 skip (vs baseline 1829/21/90 → +62 pass, -5 fail). Physics-only AST scan clean. Manifold round-trip end-to-end working (4 stacked bugs fixed: YAML 1.1 string-vs-float on density_range, example db_path, atomic_db.conn → pool, time_steps static_argnames). examples/manifold_smoke_config.yaml provides 27-spectra round-trip in ~1s for gate verification. Production manifold_config_example.yaml has 400k spectra; needs GPU. Follow-up beads filed P2/P3 for: line_mask on forward_model + Saha hoist, OLA vectorization, ChunkPlan dataclass, host.py/kernels.py file split, BayesianForwardModel._compute_spectrum kernel migration, ManifoldConfig LDM flag, 16-file black hygiene.

CF-LIBS HARD PROJECT CONSTRAINT: the FINAL algorithm delivered by this project MUST be 100% physics-based — no neural networks, no trained ML models, no PLS/PCR, no learned embeddings, no sklearn/torch/tensorflow/keras/flax/equinox/transformers/jax.nn in the production code path. ML/LLMs are ONLY allowed in the optimization/search process itself (CodeEvolve, hierarchical ES, perturbation generation, council synthesis) — they propose candidate code, they never ship with it. The evaluator must AST-scan every evolved candidate against the blocklist and reject (fitness=-inf) any that imports ML libs. See epics 3fy3 and shv for full spec.

Cluster /cluster/shared/jax-cache has uid skew — subdirs owned by nobody:hpc mode 2755 are READABLE but NOT WRITABLE by other users. XLA autotune cache writes get PERMISSION_DENIED and the error either raises (killing the job) or — worse — gets absorbed into a tight retry loop pegging Python at 100% CPU with GPU 0% for hours. Always use a USER-PRIVATE compile cache (JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR=~/jax-cache or per-user) for cluster jobs. Validated 2026-05-16: probe 1918 with private cache ran 500/1000/2 NUTS in 12.2s vs jobs 1909/1914/1915 hung indefinitely on the shared cache.

ADR-0001 implementation on integration branch feat/adr-0001-pattern-survey-impl. Per-bead specs: docs/adr/specs/T1-{1..6}-*.md. Runbook: docs/adr/ADR-0001-RUNBOOK.md. Cross-audit (2026-05-12) folded T2-3 + T2-6 into T1-1/T1-6; T2-4 retired; T2-2 narrowed to schema evolution. Wave-0 prerequisites (must merge first): 47hr baseline capture (P1), 30dq CI workflow fix (P1), oyup JAX 0.4.30+psutil pin (P2), od8m bd memory refresh (P2). Wave-1 (after wave-0): 5oar host/kernel split (absorbs T2-2 builder + T2-3 pytree registration; effort 6.5d) parallel with 14p6 lax.while_loop. Wave-2 SEQUENTIAL: e5o8 LDM broadening FIRST, then swgm forward-model unify (both touch spectrum_model.py L243-253 + batch_forward.py L395). Wave-3 parallel: ke4z chunked scan + 0mor Bayesian decomp. Subagent bootstrap sequence (runbook §3 step 0): bd memories adr-0001 → bd show <BEAD_ID> → mcp__serena__list_memories → read_memory architecture_and_data_flow. Worktree commands at .worktrees/<bead-id>/ with shared .venv symlink (forbid in-worktree pip). Sub-branches feat/adr-0001/<bead-id>-<short-name>. Docs refresh tracked by xph6.

CF-LIBS PHYSICS OPTIMIZATION ROADMAP (literature-derived, all physics-only, no ML). Top 5 highest-leverage improvements ranked by (impact × inverse effort): (1) naf2 — inverse-variance weighting of Boltzmann fits by aki_uncertainty (small, immediate accuracy + uncertainty honesty across whole pipeline). (2) 1fcg — Pace 2025 doublet-ratio SA correction as a cheap second-opinion to CDSB. (3) wzus — min >=3 lines + per-element Boltzmann R^2 gate to push identification precision past 0.7 (closes the precision regression in shv epic). (4) t2tj — joint multi-gate Saha-Boltzmann fit; reuses existing runtime/temporal infrastructure for major composition-uncertainty reduction. (5) mgp5 — Hermann two-region (core+corona) forward model; large effort but biggest expected lift on inhomogeneous matrices (slag/glass/silicate). Open frontiers per Pedarnig 2023 JAAS review: plasma inhomogeneity (covered by mgp5) and Bayesian nuisance marginalization (covered by 4w8x). Field is in a refinement phase — no pure-physics ML-free foundational solver paper appeared 2022-2026, so progress comes from orthogonal accuracy gains stacked together. All these block the shv accuracy epic.

User prefers literature-grounded, citation-bearing refactor roadmaps with explicit file targets and target functions per remediation item. Expect file paths in user's roadmaps to reflect a legacy/expected layout — always audit-map to actual paths before action. Priority framing: identification-accuracy first (#5 CDSB-at-ID > #9b Anderson > #12b A_ki RV) when ranking the optimization roadmap. Style cues: writes in section/subsection markdown with 'Target Functions' and 'Action Items' headers, expects me to cite back evidence (file:line) for any 'already done' claim.
